text:
**Two independent publish call-sites**, both going through this same codec:

- **Per-session engine** — `src/runtime.rs`. `status_for()` (`runtime.rs:97-114`) builds `domain::Status` from the live `Session` row; `enqueue_status()` (`runtime.rs:140-173`) encodes+signs it and pushes the signed JSON onto a SQLite `outbox` table (never talks to the relay directly). `spawn_outbox_drainer()` (`src/daemon/server/status_publish.rs:125-170`) drains that outbox and calls `state.transport.publish_event_checked`.
- **Daemon-wide heartbeat re-armer** — `spawn_status_heartbeat_publisher()` (`src/daemon/server/status_publish.rs:76-118`). Runs every `HEARTBEAT_SECS` (30s, `src/domain.rs:17`) over *every* alive session in the store, builds `domain::Status` via `status_from_session()` (`status_publish.rs:10-29`) from the same `Session` row, and calls `state.provider.set_status()` directly (`src/fabric/provider.rs:231-256`) — bypassing the outbox entirely.

Both read `title`/`activity`/`working` off the same `sessions` DB row and both publish independently on their own ~30s timers (see failure mode in §4). [...] - **Status outliving the session.** `mark_dead()` (`src/state/sessions.rs:419-428`) only flips `alive=0, working=0` locally — it does not publish a final/blank/expired kind:30315. Since kind:30315 carries a NIP-40 `expiration` set to `now + status_ttl` (`STATUS_TTL_SECS` = 90s, `domain.rs:14`) at the last heartbeat before death, peers reading the relay continue to see the dead session as "live" for up to 90s after it actually ended. Local readers are shielded from this by `derive_status()` (`src/session.rs:280-296`), which checks `Lifecycle::Active` and suppresses immediately — but that's a local-only guard; remote peers have no such signal and rely solely on relay-side expiration. [...] - **Two heartbeat cadences configurable independently and inconsistently.** `TENEX_EDGE_HEARTBEAT_MS` (per-engine) and the hardcoded `HEARTBEAT_SECS` constant (daemon-wide re-armer) are not the same knob — an operator tuning one believes they've changed status refresh cadence but the other loop still fires on the default.
